description: §65.9 / §65.10 / §4.15 — a `<theme>` at file top-level (a sibling of `<program>`, OUTSIDE it) is MISPLACED. `<theme>` and `<defaults>` are PROGRAM-SCOPE for v1 (an immediate child of `<program>`, a sibling of `<page>` / `<channel>`); page-scope override is deferred to v1.next. Use outside that owning locus is E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED (§65.10 reuse of §4.15). Divergence-fix proof: the recognized `<theme>` structural element was previously silently ACCEPTED at top-level (no diagnostic).
rationale: §65.9: `<theme>` is a scrml-defined structural element valid ONLY at program scope (an immediate child of `<program>`). §65.10: `<theme>`/`<defaults>` misplacement reuses E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED (§4.15/§24.4). A `<theme>` that parses to a theme-decl node but sits anywhere other than a direct child of a `<program>` node (file top-level here, or inside `<page>`) fires the structural-placement error. It is a clean, single-cause diagnosis — NOT a parse-confusion cascade (no E-CTX-003 / E-SYNTAX-002) and NOT the incidental E-COMPONENT-* family.
